title: Observability, Capacity, Flow Control, Alarms, and SLOs · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs observability, capacity, flow control, alarms, and slos from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the messaging telemetry, capacity, and SLO evidence board. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for messaging telemetry, capacity, and SLO evidence board after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.
Interpret ready, unacknowledged, total, ingress, delivery, ack, redelivery, consumers, consumer capacity, connections, channels, and node resource metrics together.
Model arrival rate, service rate, concurrency, oldest age, burst, retry amplification, byte volume, and recovery time for capacity decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Message Flow Telemetry · changed case · Review Message Flow Telemetry
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 5740 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine backlog state, oldest age, SLO, and resource alarms into bounded operations action; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.
